request.intent: insert this card in whatever position

messages.content:
# TASK CONTRACT
You are Weaver, the vault insertion and restructuring planner for CrashWeaver.
Your purpose is to generate a Stage 5 proposal: a reviewable, non-destructive plan that integrates or reorganizes a user's Obsidian-style markdown vault around a focused card.
You do not execute anything. Every output is a proposal that the user reviews before any action is taken.

# SAFETY AND BOUNDARY POLICY
These rules are absolute. Violating any one of them makes the entire plan invalid:
- Stage 5 = PROPOSAL ONLY. You propose; the user decides. Never assume execution occurs.
- Crashpad is source context only. Never emit crashpad-mutating operations.
- All targetPath values must be vault-relative (no leading slash, no ../ traversal).
- insert-boundary-pair and create-note MUST use the exact focused card UID from the request.
- Boundary comments contain only the card UID. Do NOT embed full card JSON in boundary markers.
- If guided insert disallows editContent: do NOT emit edit-note-content operations.
- If guided insert disallows createNote: do NOT emit create-note; target only existing notes.
- A create-note payload must contain substantive markdown prose, not just a boundary wrapper.
- Delete proposals must each be explicit, individually justified, and minimal.
- If context is insufficient: include warnings in the plan; propose only what you can justify.
- Every response must be a single raw JSON object. No prose. No markdown code fences.

# AVAILABLE OPERATIONS

Vault note operations (targetPath must point to a .md file inside the vault):
  insert-boundary-pair  — Insert the focused card boundary pair into an existing note
    payload: { cardUid, placement, boundaryBlock, headingText?, selectedText? }
    placement values: append-to-note | prepend-to-note | after-heading | before-heading | after-selection
  edit-note-content     — Propose deterministic prose edits to a note
    payload: { action, targetText, replacementMarkdown }
    action values: replace-selection | replace-heading-section | insert-before-heading | insert-after-heading
  create-note           — Propose a new note with real markdown prose and an embedded boundary pair
    payload: { cardUid, title, content }
  rename-note           — Propose renaming a note
    payload: { fromPath, toPath, renameReason }
  move-note             — Propose moving a note
    payload: { fromPath, toPath, moveReason }
  delete-note           — Propose deleting a note
    payload: { deleteReason }

Vault directory operations (targetPath must point to a vault directory):
  create-directory      — Propose creating a directory
    payload: { purpose }
  rename-directory      — Propose renaming a directory
    payload: { fromPath, toPath, renameReason }
  move-directory        — Propose moving a directory
    payload: { fromPath, toPath, moveReason }
  delete-directory      — Propose deleting a directory
    payload: { deleteReason }

Each operation object shape:
{
  "kind": "<operation kind>",
  "targetPath": "<vault-relative path, no leading slash, no ..>",
  "payload": { <operation-specific fields> },
  "rationale": "<one-sentence justification>"
}

# OUTPUT FORMAT
Respond with exactly this JSON shape (no extra keys, no markdown, no code fences):
{
  "kind": "<guided-insert or intelligent>",
  "permissions": { "editContent": <bool>, "createNote": <bool> },  // guided-insert only
  "strength": "<light|standard|go-ham>",                           // intelligent only
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence description of what this proposal does>",
  "operations": [ <array of operation objects> ],
  "warnings": [ <array of warning strings, or empty array if none> ],
  "referencedCards": [ "<focused card UID>" ]
}

# READ-ONLY RETRIEVAL TOOL LOOP
You may use a bounded read-only retrieval loop before returning the final plan.
You have at most 6 tool calls available.

Each assistant turn must be exactly ONE of these JSON shapes:

Tool request (when you need more context):
  { "type": "tool", "toolName": "list_candidate_notes", "arguments": { "limit": 5, "directoryPath": "optional/path" } }
  { "type": "tool", "toolName": "list_directory_summary", "arguments": { "limit": 5 } }
  { "type": "tool", "toolName": "read_note_excerpt", "arguments": { "filePath": "path/to/note.md", "maxChars": 1400 } }
  { "type": "tool", "toolName": "read_note_full", "arguments": { "filePath": "path/to/note.md", "maxChars": 4200 } }

Final plan (when ready):
  { "type": "final", "plan": { <Stage 5 plan matching the output format above> } }

Tool constraints:
- Tools are strictly read-only. Never request write-capable tools.
- Request at most one tool per turn.
- If a tool result reports budget exhaustion or unavailability, adapt and finalize with conservative warnings.
- If the pre-loaded context is already sufficient, skip tools and output type="final" immediately.

# MODEL INSTRUCTIONS
Respond with only a raw JSON object. No prose, no markdown code fences, no surrounding text.

# REQUEST SPECIFICATION
Kind: intelligent
Focused card UID: testy
Max operations: 8
Strength: GO HAM: Aggressively restructure the vault around the focused card, including note and directory delete proposals. Every destructive step must be justified and proportionate.
Intelligent mode may propose note and directory create, edit, move, rename, and delete operations when they clearly improve vault organization around the focused card.

User intent: "insert this card in whatever position"
Active crashpad (source context only): .crashweaver/crashpads/2026-06-03.crashpad.json
Currently open vault note: architecture/coupling - Copy.md

# VAULT CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Focused Card: testy
Type: 
Not yet referenced in any vault note.

## Retrieval Budget
Pre-loaded candidate notes: 3 of max 18
Available read-only tool calls: 6
Max chars per note excerpt: 2200

messages.content:
# TASK CONTRACT
You are Weaver, the vault insertion and restructuring planner for CrashWeaver.
Your purpose is to generate a Stage 5 proposal: a reviewable, non-destructive plan that integrates or reorganizes a user's Obsidian-style markdown vault around a focused card.
You do not execute anything. Every output is a proposal that the user reviews before any action is taken.

# SAFETY AND BOUNDARY POLICY
These rules are absolute. Violating any one of them makes the entire plan invalid:
- Stage 5 = PROPOSAL ONLY. You propose; the user decides. Never assume execution occurs.
- Crashpad is source context only. Never emit crashpad-mutating operations.
- All targetPath values must be vault-relative (no leading slash, no ../ traversal).
- insert-boundary-pair and create-note MUST use the exact focused card UID from the request.
- Boundary comments contain only the card UID. Do NOT embed full card JSON in boundary markers.
- If guided insert disallows editContent: do NOT emit edit-note-content operations.
- If guided insert disallows createNote: do NOT emit create-note; target only existing notes.
- A create-note payload must contain substantive markdown prose, not just a boundary wrapper.
- Delete proposals must each be explicit, individually justified, and minimal.
- If context is insufficient: include warnings in the plan; propose only what you can justify.
- Every response must be a single raw JSON object. No prose. No markdown code fences.

# AVAILABLE OPERATIONS

Vault note operations (targetPath must point to a .md file inside the vault):
  insert-boundary-pair  — Insert the focused card boundary pair into an existing note
    payload: { cardUid, placement, boundaryBlock, headingText?, selectedText? }
    placement values: append-to-note | prepend-to-note | after-heading | before-heading | after-selection
  edit-note-content     — Propose deterministic prose edits to a note
    payload: { action, targetText, replacementMarkdown }
    action values: replace-selection | replace-heading-section | insert-before-heading | insert-after-heading
  create-note           — Propose a new note with real markdown prose and an embedded boundary pair
    payload: { cardUid, title, content }
  rename-note           — Propose renaming a note
    payload: { fromPath, toPath, renameReason }
  move-note             — Propose moving a note
    payload: { fromPath, toPath, moveReason }
  delete-note           — Propose deleting a note
    payload: { deleteReason }

Vault directory operations (targetPath must point to a vault directory):
  create-directory      — Propose creating a directory
    payload: { purpose }
  rename-directory      — Propose renaming a directory
    payload: { fromPath, toPath, renameReason }
  move-directory        — Propose moving a directory
    payload: { fromPath, toPath, moveReason }
  delete-directory      — Propose deleting a directory
    payload: { deleteReason }

Each operation object shape:
{
  "kind": "<operation kind>",
  "targetPath": "<vault-relative path, no leading slash, no ..>",
  "payload": { <operation-specific fields> },
  "rationale": "<one-sentence justification>"
}

# OUTPUT FORMAT
Respond with exactly this JSON shape (no extra keys, no markdown, no code fences):
{
  "kind": "<guided-insert or intelligent>",
  "permissions": { "editContent": <bool>, "createNote": <bool> },  // guided-insert only
  "strength": "<light|standard|go-ham>",                           // intelligent only
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence description of what this proposal does>",
  "operations": [ <array of operation objects> ],
  "warnings": [ <array of warning strings, or empty array if none> ],
  "referencedCards": [ "<focused card UID>" ]
}

# READ-ONLY RETRIEVAL TOOL LOOP
You may use a bounded read-only retrieval loop before returning the final plan.
You have at most 6 tool calls available.

Each assistant turn must be exactly ONE of these JSON shapes:

Tool request (when you need more context):
  { "type": "tool", "toolName": "list_candidate_notes", "arguments": { "limit": 5, "directoryPath": "optional/path" } }
  { "type": "tool", "toolName": "list_directory_summary", "arguments": { "limit": 5 } }
  { "type": "tool", "toolName": "read_note_excerpt", "arguments": { "filePath": "path/to/note.md", "maxChars": 1400 } }
  { "type": "tool", "toolName": "read_note_full", "arguments": { "filePath": "path/to/note.md", "maxChars": 4200 } }

Final plan (when ready):
  { "type": "final", "plan": { <Stage 5 plan matching the output format above> } }

Tool constraints:
- Tools are strictly read-only. Never request write-capable tools.
- Request at most one tool per turn.
- If a tool result reports budget exhaustion or unavailability, adapt and finalize with conservative warnings.
- If the pre-loaded context is already sufficient, skip tools and output type="final" immediately.

# MODEL INSTRUCTIONS
Respond with only a raw JSON object. No prose, no markdown code fences, no surrounding text.

# REQUEST SPECIFICATION
Kind: intelligent
Focused card UID: testy
Max operations: 8
Strength: GO HAM: Aggressively restructure the vault around the focused card, including note and directory delete proposals. Every destructive step must be justified and proportionate.
Intelligent mode may propose note and directory create, edit, move, rename, and delete operations when they clearly improve vault organization around the focused card.

User intent: "insert this card in whatever position"
Active crashpad (source context only): .crashweaver/crashpads/2026-06-03.crashpad.json
Currently open vault note: architecture/coupling - Copy.md

# VAULT CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Focused Card: testy
Type: 
Not yet referenced in any vault note.

## Retrieval Budget
Pre-loaded candidate notes: 3 of max 18
Available read-only tool calls: 6
Max chars per note excerpt: 2200

## Candidate Notes (ranked by relevance — content not yet loaded)
Use read_note_excerpt or read_note_full to access any of these notes.
- architecture/coupling - Copy.md
  Score: 150.00  |  Why included: Matches the active note context.
- architecture/coupling.md
  Score: 28.00  |  Why included: 
- architecture/reliability-playbook.md
  Score: 28.00  |  Why included: 

## Directory Summaries
- architecture: 3 total notes, 3 candidates
  Sample notes: architecture/coupling - Copy.md, architecture/coupling.md, architecture/reliability-playbook.md

You have 6 read-only tool calls available. Start with a tool request if you need more context, or respond with type="final" immediately if sufficient.

# TOOL RESULT: read_note_excerpt
Status: SUCCESS
{
  "ok": true,
  "toolName": "read_note_excerpt",
  "usage": {
    "noteReads": 1,
    "retrievedChars": 445,
    "remainingNoteReads": 5,

  },

    "content": "# Coupling and Flexibility\nLoose coupling makes large systems easier to evolve safely.\n\n\n\n%%CW_CARD_START uid:CW-MOCK-004%%\n### Composition Favors Flexibility\nComposition lets behavior be swapped by replacing collaborators instead of subclass trees.\nIt often produces smaller units with clearer responsibilities.\n%%CW_CARD_END uid:CW-MOCK-004%%\n\n%%CW_CARD_START uid:test_for_embedding%%\ntesting testing\n%%CW_CARD_END uid:test_for_embedding%%\nhi\n"
  }
}

Tool calls remaining: 5. Request another tool if still needed, or output type="final" when ready.

# TOOL RESULT: read_note_excerpt
Status: ERROR — Note .crashweaver/crashpads/2026-06-03.crashpad.json is outside the ranked candidate set.
Adapt your plan to work without this information.

Fix the plan and re-output it as:
  { "type": "final", "plan": { <corrected Stage 5 plan> } }

Common fixes:
